# 💸 Finance (177)

Where AI meets money on the line. From bots that trade while you sleep to models that crunch alpha out of noise, these repos turn market data into edge — and the stakes make every line of code count.

## Algorithmic Trading

Strategy engines, backtesters, and live execution bots that turn signals into fills

## Quant Research

Factor models, signal mining, and statistical toolkits for hunting alpha in the noise

<details>
<summary>Show 14 repos</summary>

- [goldmansachs/gs-quant](https://github.com/goldmansachs/gs-quant) — ⭐ 11,131 · `Python` · Library — gs-quant is a Python toolkit developed by Goldman Sachs for quantitative finance, providing tools for derivatives, risk management, and trading strategies.
- [dcajasn/Riskfolio-Lib](https://github.com/dcajasn/Riskfolio-Lib) — ⭐ 4,179 · `C++` · Library — Riskfolio-Lib is a Python library focused on portfolio optimization and quantitative strategic asset allocation. It provides tools for managing investment risk and constructing efficient portfolios for quantitative finance applications.
- [Mathieu2301/TradingView-API](https://github.com/Mathieu2301/TradingView-API) — ⭐ 3,557 · `JavaScript` · Library — A JavaScript library to get real-time stock and cryptocurrency data, charts, and indicators directly from TradingView for purposes like backtesting trading strategies.
- [skfolio/skfolio](https://github.com/skfolio/skfolio) — ⭐ 1,959 · `Python` · Library — Skfolio is a Python library for portfolio optimization and risk management, built on top of scikit-learn. It provides tools for asset allocation, quantitative finance strategies, and efficient frontier analysis.
- [microsoft/MarS](https://github.com/microsoft/MarS) — ⭐ 1,707 · `Python` · Framework — MarS is a financial market simulation engine powered by generative foundation models, designed to simulate financial markets.
- [QuantaAlpha/QuantaAlpha](https://github.com/QuantaAlpha/QuantaAlpha) — ⭐ 1,094 · `Python` · Application — QuantaAlpha is an application that discovers quantitative alpha factors by combining LLM intelligence with evolutionary strategies, automatically mining, evolving, and validating factors from a described research direction.
- [Limex-com/ziplime](https://github.com/Limex-com/ziplime) — ⭐ 445 · `Python` · Library — ZipLime is a Python library designed for quantitative finance, reinventing the classic backtesting experience for algorithmic trading strategies. It serves as a tool for financial researchers and developers to test and analyze trading models.
